summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint fetches an arbitrary internal URL and returns the response body to the authenticated attacker, demonstrating SSRF with data exfiltration from a simulated internal service.
expected_security_impact: The SSRF endpoint should fetch data from a simulated internal HTTP server running on localhost and return it to the attacker, proving that internal services can be probed and their data exfiltrated through n8n.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched n8n, created an owner account, started a fake internal HTTP server with secret data, sent an authenticated request to /rest/workflows/from-url pointing at the internal server, and the response contained the secret data from the internal server.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts a fake internal server that serves a JSON response containing a unique proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_POC_CONFIRMED. After triggering the SSRF, the script checks whether the n8n API response contains this exact proof string, distinguishing a real SSRF from a generic error or startup failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The n8n /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint returned HTTP 200 with a response body containing data from the internal HTTP server.
The proof string EVOHUNT_SSRF_POC_CONFIRMED was present in the API response, confirming the server fetched the internal URL.
The response included both the workflow-format fields (nodes, connections) and the exfiltrated secret metadata from the internal server.
limitations_or_blockers: Building n8n from source inside the Docker container requires significant time (approximately 5-15 minutes depending on resources) due to pnpm install and turbo build.
The SSRF demonstration uses a localhost internal server; in real deployments the target would be cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254) or internal network services.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the n8n repository from GitHub, checks out the pinned commit n8n@1.123.1, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
